data.name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

data.flavor_text: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

data.name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

data.flavor_text: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

data.name: Charizard

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.text: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are Fire Energy instead of their usual type. This Pokémon Power stops working if this Pokémon is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

data.flavor_text: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

data.name: Ponyta
data.flavor_text: These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.

data.name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

data.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

data.name: Magmar

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavor_text: Found at the mouths of volcanoes and extremely hard to spot. There are very few instances of capturing this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Phoenix Breath
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your  Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Eternal Blaze
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

data.name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

data.flavor_text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

data.name: Raichu
data.flavor_text: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

data.name: Voltorb
data.flavor_text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

data.name: Electrode

abilities.name: Buzzap
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may Knock Out this Pokémon and attach it to 1 of your other Pokémon as a Special Energy card. While this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time. This Pokémon Power can't be used if this Pokémon is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

data.flavor_text: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

data.name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavor_text: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.

data.name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

data.flavor_text: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

data.name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.flavor_text: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Strike and Run
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you put any Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, you may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sudden Flash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.name: Stantler

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Frighten Horn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is now Confused.

data.flavor_text: Staring at its antlers creates an odd sensation as if one were being drawn into their centers.

data.name: Miltank

attacks.name: Healing Milk
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Pokémon, and then flip 2 coins. For each heads, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

data.flavor_text: It is said that kids who drink MILTANK's milk grow up to become hearty, healthy adults.

data.name: Boss's Orders
rules: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

data.name: Computer Search
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.name: Pokémon Fan Club
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.name: Professor Oak
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

data.name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

data.name: Rocket's Admin
rules: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of their remaining Prize cards.

data.name: Scorching Charcoal
rules: The attacks used by the Charizard this card is attached to do 100 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.name: Super Rod
rules: Shuffle up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

data.name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

data.name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

data.name: Ultra Ball
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.name: VS Seeker
rules: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

data.name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 Colorless Energy.
